questions.questionText:
Which one of the following pesticides sprayed
over cashew plantations has been linked to structural deformities in children?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Endosulfan** is a chlorinated cyclodiene organochlorine pesticide, marketed under names like “Thiodan”. It is a **persistent, lipophilic compound** which bioaccumulates and biomagnifies.
● Endosulfan can enter the air, water, and soil in regions where it is used, leading to environmental contamination. It is applied in agriculture to manage pests like whiteflies, aphids, beetles, and worms. Additionally, it serves as a wood preservative.
● **Traditionally sprayed on crops such as cotton, cashew, tea, tobacco, and various vegetables to** **control pests like aphids, whiteflies, and beetles.** It may **persist in the surface soil for weeks to** **months** after application. Its degradation half-life ranges from weeks to years in soil and sediment and is highly toxic to aquatic life, including fish and invertebrates.
● After entering the human body, it acts on the central nervous system through GABA-receptor antagonism, causing convulsions, neurotoxicity, and endocrine disruption.  Human exposure via ingestion or inhalation is linked to conditions such as birth deformities, neurodevelopmental delays, cancer, and reproductive toxicity.
● It is considered a legacy pollutant and its persistent residues continue to pose environmental and health risks in previously used areas.  **It was Banned in India in 2011, and globally by the 2011 Stockholm** **Convention as a Persistent Organic Pollutant (POP)** and listed under the Rotterdam Convention to ensure prior informed consent before international trade. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Environment

questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
Agroforestry:
I. 	 It is a land-use system that diversify
farm outputs and enhances ecological sustainability.
II. 	 It conserves moisture by creating a humid
microclimate.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Agroforestry** is a land-use system that integrates trees and shrubs with agricultural crops and/or livestock on the same unit of land, either simultaneously or sequentially. It is designed to maximize productivity, diversify farm outputs, and enhance ecological sustainability. **So, statement I is correct.**
● It aims to improve **soil health**, **enhance biodiversity, increase carbon sequestration, and ensure** **livelihood resilience**. The primary goal of agroforestry systems is to maximize positive interactions between trees, crops, livestock, and the environment to create a diversified and sustainable production system. Key benefits include soil fertility improvement through better nutrient cycling, increased organic matter, and enhanced soil structure.
o **Nutrient Cycling:** Deep-rooted trees access nutrients from lower soil horizons that annual crops cannot reach. Through **litter fall (leaves, twigs)** and **root turnover**, these nutrients are brought back to the topsoil. Microorganisms decompose the litter, making nutrients like **nitrogen (N),** **phosphorus (P), and potassium (K)** available for crops. **So, statement I is correct.** o **Organic Matter Enrichment:** Trees shed leaves seasonally or year-round, increasing **soil organic** **carbon** and humus content. Organic matter enhances soil structure, supports microbial diversity, and improves **cation exchange capacity (CEC)**—making nutrients more available.
o **Improved Soil Structure:** Tree roots improve **soil aggregation** and **porosity**, reducing compaction. Better soil structure enhances **infiltration, aeration**, and **root penetration** of companion crops.
● The tree canopies reduce sunlight exposure and evaporation, while leaf litter acts as mulch, conserving moisture and improving water infiltration. Additionally, tree roots improve soil structure and porosity, increasing the soil’s ability to hold water.
● **These systems also reduce surface runoff and create a cooler, more humid microclimate**, all of which contribute to better moisture conservation especially in dry and rainfed regions. **So, statement II is** **correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:**
Millets are being promoted for cultivation as a
part of nutrition and food security.
**Statement II:**
Millets have a higher productivity (Kg/ha) than
wheat and rice.
**Statement III:**
Millets are leguminous plants enhancing the
nitrogen fixation to soils.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement II and Statement III are
correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement II and Statement III are
correct, but only one of them explains
Statement I
options.c:
Only one of the Statements II and III is correct
and that explains Statement I
options.d:
Neither Statement II nor Statement III is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Millets** are a group of small grained cereal food crops which are highly nutritious. They are rainfed, **hardy** **grains which have low requirements of water and fertility** when compared to other popular cereals like wheat and rice.
● Millets are rich in **dietary fiber, essential amino acids, vitamins, and minerals** like iron, magnesium, and phosphorus. They offer **better protein quality** than some cereals like maize and have a **lower** **glycemic index (~52.7),** aiding in blood sugar control. Their soluble and insoluble fibers support digestion, and their nutrient density helps combat malnutrition in carbohydrate-heavy diets.
● Rice, maize, and wheat supply 60% of global food energy, but rely heavily on water and climate conditions. Millets, by contrast, thrive in semi-arid regions, needing less water and inputs. They are **70%** **more water-efficient than rice**, tolerate heat up to 42°C, and offer a resilient, food-secure option for drought-prone tropical areas. **Millets have a much lower carbon footprint than rice and wheat, making** **them environmentally sustainable. Hence millets are being promoted for cultivation as a part of** **nutrition and food security.  So, statement I is correct.**
● While millets are known for their resilience and nutritional value, **the productivity of wheat and rice** **mostly exceed that of millets**, especially when modern farming practices are used. Millets are often grown in less fertile or drought-prone areas, which naturally reduces their yield potential. **Also, millets** **have a shorter shelf life than rice and wheat,** requiring specific storage conditions to prevent spoilage. **So, Statement II is not correct.**
● **Millets are not leguminous crops.** They are classified as cereals, specifically belonging to the grass family (Poaceae). Leguminous crops, like pulses, have the unique ability to fix atmospheric nitrogen into the soil, enhancing soil fertility. **So, Statement III is not correct.**
o **Leguminous crops** are those which belong to the pea family (Leguminosae). Plants in this family generally **have root nodules hosting rhizobium bacteria that fix nitrogen from the** **atmosphere**. **So, neither Statement–II nor Statement–III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.questionText:
With reference to Zero Budget Natural Farming
practices, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It does not involve any cost for farming as it
eliminates the cost of external inputs.
II. 	 It seeks to preserve the natural agro-ecosystem
by minimizing human intervention.
III. 	ZBNF and Organic farming involve the same
approach towards field cultivation.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**
● The word ‘budget’ refers to credit and expenses, thus the phrase 'Zero Budget' means without using any credit, and without spending any money on purchased inputs. 'Natural farming' means farming with Nature and without chemicals. Therefore, **Zero Budget Natural Farming** (ZBNF) doesn’t mean that the farmer
is going to have no costs at all, but rather that any costs will be compensated for by income from intercrops, **making farming a close to zero budget activity. So, statement I is not correct.**
● ZBNF aims to eliminate the cost of external inputs like fertilizers and pesticides by leveraging natural processes and local resources. It utilizes techniques like:
o **Bijamrita****:** Seed treatment using cow dung, cow urine, and lime. o **Jivamrita****:** A fermented microbial culture made from cow dung, cow urine, jaggery, pulse flour, and soil. o **Ghana jivamrita****:** A solid form of jivamrita, used as a soil amendment. o **Achhadana** **(Mulching):** Using crop residues to cover the soil, which helps retain moisture and suppress weeds. o **Whapasa****:** Maintaining soil moisture through various techniques, including mulching.
● Although Zero Budget Natural Farming (ZBNF) and organic farming share the common goal of cultivating crops without synthetic fertilizers and chemical pesticides, they differ significantly in their approach. Organic farming permits interventions such as ploughing, tilling, and the use of bulk manures to enhance soil fertility and crop yield. **So, statement III is not correct.**
● In contrast, **natural farming seeks to preserve the natural agro-ecosystem by minimizing human** **intervention** avoiding practices like ploughing, tilling, weeding, and the application of bulk manures thus maintaining soil structure and promoting ecological balance. **So, statement II is correct.** **So, only two of the statements given above are correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Soils of India
questions.questionText:
With reference to the salinization of soils in
Northern India, consider the following statements:
**Statement I:**
Salinization has adversely affected the productivity
of soil in Northern India.
**Statement II:**
Flood irrigation is the predominant irrigation
technique in this region.
**Statements III:**
There has been an increasing use of chemical
fertilizers for cultivation in this region.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement II and Statement III are
correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.c:
Only one of the Statements II and III is correct
and that explains Statement I
options.d:
Neither Statement II nor Statement III is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Soil salinization** means the accumulation of water-soluble salts in the soil. It is a major process of land degradation that decreases the soil fertility productivity. Salinization of soil negatively impacts plant development and induces land degradation. In India, soil salinity leads to a loss of 16.8 million metric tons of crops every year.
○ **In India, approximately 6.73 million hectares of land are affected by soil salinity. This** **constitutes around 2.1% of the country's total geographical area.** The most affected states include Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, West Bengal, and Rajasthan. **So, statement I is** **correct.**
● Soil salinization is caused by inappropriate irrigation practices. Excess irrigation with poor drainage can lead to soil salinization. When irrigation is not done properly, and excess water is left behind, this leads to water logging. **As the water evaporates, it leaves salt behind**. In India, soil has been getting saltier in an era of rising sea levels and heavy floods. Floodwater events led to elevated salt levels in the soil's topmost layer.
● Surface irrigation or flood irrigation is a traditional method in which water moves over the land, depending on the gravitational gradient. When water is applied to the field, it advances across the surface until the water extends over the entire area. Flood irrigation leads to soil fertility loss due to waterlogging and salinity. Flood irrigation has been the most common method of irrigating agricultural land.
○ **Flood irrigation is still widely practiced in Northern India**. This method **leads to waterlogging,**
**which contributes to salinization** due to capillary action bringing salts to the surface. **So,** **statement II is correct.**
● **Some fertilizers contain high levels of salts such as potassium chloride or ammonium sulfate.** Spreading large quantities of such fertilizers increases soil salinity, which harms crops and crop productivity.
○ In India, farmers' consumption of chemical fertilizers has increased by a staggering 95% between 2000 and 2021. This has led to increased salinisation of soil. **So, statement III is correct.**
● Climate change is another reason. In a warming world, melting glaciers are boosting sea levels, which is also boosting soil salinity as saltwater seeps into coastal lands.
● Overexploitation of ground water is also leading to soil salinisation. Increasingly extreme heat waves have been drying up water sources, forcing farmers to pump out more groundwater. It brings salts to the soil surface where they get precipitated when water evaporates. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Impact of Soil Salinization:**
● Saline Soils have difficulties in the exchange of water and nutrients with the roots of plants. Salinity thus decreases the agricultural production of most crops.
● It affects soil physicochemical properties, and ecological balance of the area.
● Soil salinity imposes ion toxicity, osmotic stress, nutrient (N, Ca, K, P, Fe, Zn) deficiency and oxidative stress on plants.
● It limits water uptake from soil.
● Soil salinity significantly reduces plant phosphorus (P) uptake because phosphate ions precipitate with Ca ions.
● Salinity also affects photosynthesis mainly through a reduction in leaf area, chlorophyll content and stomatal conductance.

questions.questionText: What is ‘trash farming’?

options.a:
Cultivation of crops adversely affecting the
underground water tables
options.b:
Cultivation of crops primarily for increasing
natural carbon sequestration
options.c:
Cultivation of crops using household organic
wastes as manure
options.d:
Cultivation of crops with the purpose of
covering the soil with mulch

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Mulching is the practice of covering the soil surface with organic materials like plant residues** with an aim to conserve soil moisture, avoid runoff and increase soil productivity.
● **Trash farming is a practice where the crop residues are cut, chopped, and mixed into the ground to** **act as mulch.** It is a modified form of mulching, where crop residues like stubble are left on the soil surface to act as a mulch.
● Trash farming **helps control soil erosion and adds organic matter to the soil.** It also **improves** **microbial biodiversity in the soil.** Also, it allows for **efficient water use and possibly reduces the use** **of external inputs like fertilizers**. On a no-till farm, it increases water infiltration, conserving moisture while reducing excess evaporation.
● It slows down the intensity of rainfall before it contacts the soil surface, preventing soil erosion and surface run-off. **It provides a cover to base crops, making them more resilient.** Mulching/trash farming also reduces evaporation, checks weeds, and protects the crops against external weather impact. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:** In North India, people feel much more heat in late August and early September than in late March and early April, even at the same atmospheric temperature.
**Statement II:** The wet bulb temperature is lower in the late monsoon season than in early summer.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct,
but Statement II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Summer season which extends from March to May months in Northern India** is characterized by elevated atmospheric temperatures along with warm but dry winds. This summer season is succeeded by the **monsoon season, which is characterized by high relative humidity of the ai**r.
● The temperature ranges during the summer and the monsoon months is largely the same, still it is during the latter when people report to experience greater heat than during the peak summer months. **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● This is because, **in humans just like ungulates (cows, horses etc.) sweating is the primary response** **for lowering the body temperature**. Sweating works on the principle of evaporation, wherein water in the form of sweat is perspired on the surface of the body, this water then evaporates using heat from the body, thus leading to a cooling effect.
● The hotter it is, the more strain our bodies feel, and the more we need to sweat to cool down. **But humid** **air has less capacity to hold additional moisture**, so water evaporates more slowly in humid conditions. **Humidity reduces the body’s ability to cool itself through sweating.**
● **Wet-bulb temperature** is the lowest temperature to which an object can cool down when moisture evaporates from it. **The lower the wet-bulb temperature, the easier it is for us to cool down.** It measures how well our bodies cool down by sweating.
○ A wet-bulb temperature of 35°C is considered the maximum limit of humidity that humans can handle. **Beyond this, the body can no longer effectively cool itself via perspiration.** ○ As long as the wet-bulb temperature is well below your skin temperature, your body can release heat to your surroundings through radiation and sweating. But as the wet-bulb temperature approaches your core temperature, you lose the ability to cool down. ○ In contrast to dry heat, most extreme wet bulb temperature events in India take place on rainy days during the monsoon season. This **increase in wet bulb temperature hampers the ability of the** **body to cool down through sweating**. **So, statement II is correct.** **So, both Statement–I and Statement–II are correct and Statement–II explains Statement–I.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
